summary: The term "vulcanization" describes a process involving irreversible changes via heat treatment which results in increased rigidity; among provided choices, zinc oxide-eugenol cements exemplify such characteristics after being subjected to appropriate temperatures during their cure cycle. Agent2 reasoned by evaluating how closely each choice matched the definition of vulcanization based primarily on whether it involved an irreversible transformation under thermal conditions typical of true vulcanization processes observed clinically or scientifically documented within dentistry contexts.
